```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu_hat <- round(runif(1, 3, 9), 2)
sigma  <- sample(c(1, 2, 3), 1)
n      <- sample(c(36, 49, 64, 100), 1)

## Respostas: limite inferior do IC unilateral 95%
correct <- round(mu_hat - 1.645 * sigma / sqrt(n), 4)
w1 <- round(mu_hat - 1.96 * sigma / sqrt(n), 4)
w2 <- round(mu_hat + 1.645 * sigma / sqrt(n), 4)
w3 <- round(mu_hat - 1.645 * sigma / n, 4)
w4 <- round(mu_hat - 1.282 * sigma / sqrt(n), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
O limite inferior do intervalo de confianca unilateral 95% para $\mu$ e $\bar{X} - z_{0{,}95}\,\sigma/\sqrt{n}$, com $z_{0{,}95} = 1{,}645$. Com $\bar{X} = `r mu_hat`$, $\sigma = `r sigma`$ e $n = `r n`$, o limite inferior e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
O limite inferior e $\bar{X} - 1{,}645\,\sigma/\sqrt{n} = `r mu_hat` - 1{,}645 \times `r sigma` / \sqrt{`r n`} = `r correct`$.

Erros comuns: usar $z_{0{,}975}=1{,}96$ (bilateral); somar em vez de subtrair (limite superior); dividir por $n$ em vez de $\sqrt{n}$; usar $z_{0{,}90}=1{,}282$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S1Q8_IC_unilateral
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
